name: Attached architecture spec is read and injected into the Project-context prompt slot (AC-35, e2e half)
description: The Security Reviewer has the architectural-invariant spec 'specs/architecture.md' attached (seeded on the agent's attached_doc_paths) and PR #501 has a seeded, completed Security Reviewer run whose trace shows the spec was read and injected. From PR #501, open the Agent runs tab, open the seeded run's trace drawer, and confirm the spec path appears in the trace's 'Specs read' row and the assembled '## Project context' prompt block (labelled 'Project context — attached specs (untrusted)') contains the invariant text. Per risk R-8 (docs/plans/project-context.md), agent-browser is deterministic with no LLM, so this flow asserts injection visibility only — it does NOT assert on any LLM-generated finding text. The companion assertion that a grounded finding survives groundFindings() lives in the hermetic server test server/src/modules/reviews/project-context-injection.test.ts (T16) via MockLLMProvider. Read-only seeded data (no model call). Run locally via the hermetic stack (see e2e/README.md).

cmd: Query the users table directly from the public API handler
steps.label: seeded violating PR title row is visible

cmd: Query the users table directly from the public API handler
steps.label: open the PR row
navigate to the PR detail route
switch to the Agent runs tab
findings tab is active in the URL

the PR's run timeline section renders (the seeded Security Reviewer run); rendered upper-case via CSS text-transform on SectionLabel, so the assertion matches the rendered case, not the raw 'Timeline' prop
open the run trace drawer for the seeded run (icon-only button, accessible via its aria-label)
trace drawer param is set in the URL
the attached spec path appears in the trace's Specs read row
scroll the (below-the-fold) Prompt assembly section into view inside the drawer
expand the Prompt assembly trace section

the assembled Project-context block contains the invariant text (backtick-free substring of 'Module `api/` must not import `db/` directly' — agent-browser's --text matcher mishandles literal backticks, so we assert on the backtick-free crux of the invariant, which is equally diagnostic that the spec was injected)
